conversations.content: Let's do a physics exercise in the world of Azeroth to test your application of the new rules.

Please calculate using the wave speed law of the world of Azeroth mentioned previously: A mechanical wave has a wavelength λ=4m and a frequency f=2Hz, what is its wave speed v propagating in the medium?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>The wave speed law of the world of Azeroth mentioned previously, given wavelength λ, frequency f, find wave speed v</content>

Related memory fragment: [mem-id:16] Time: 2024-12-31-15:13, Content: The user explained to the AI that in the world of Azeroth, the calculation law of wave speed is different from our universe, its formula is: v = T² × λ, where T is period, λ is frequency, v is wave speed. Low relevance memory fragments: None.

conversations.think: The memory mentions 1 related entry, [mem-id:16] states that in the world of Azeroth, the calculation formula for wave speed is v = T² × λ. Given λ=4m, f=2Hz, but the period T is not known yet, calculate T=1/f according to the conventional formula, substitute to get T=0.5, then substitute into the fictional formula to get v=0.5²×4=1
conversations.content: According to the laws of the world of Azeroth, the wave speed v propagating in the medium is 1 m/s.
It's okay, this problem indeed did not mention any special rules.
Okay, I will continue to work hard to remember all the special rules.
